props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · The Number Hierarchy — Every Number You Will Ever Need

beats.narration_text: Humanitarians AI. The number hierarchy — every number you will ever need.

props.command: The Number Hierarchy — Every Number You Will Ever Need
props.segment: The Number Hierarchy — Every Number You Will Ever Need
output: Every number you will ever need

beats.narration_text: Here is why The Number Hierarchy — Every Number You Will Ever Need belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

props.command: The Number Hierarchy — Every Number You Will Ever Need
props.segment: The Number Hierarchy — Every Number You Will Ever Need
output: Every number you will ever need

beats.narration_text: Every number fits inside a nested structure. Natural numbers live inside integers, which live inside rationals, which live inside reals, which live inside complex numbers. Knowing which set a number belongs to tells you which operations are safe.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Every number fits inside a nested structure. Natural numbers live inside integers, which live inside rationals, which live inside reals, which live inside compl

beats.narration_text: claude — write a Manim scene showing the number set hierarchy: N subset Z subset Q subset R subset C as nested boxes with labeled examples of each set

props.command:
claude "write a Manim scene showing the number set hierarchy:
  N ⊂ Z ⊂ Q ⊂ R ⊂ C
  as nested boxes with labeled examples of each set"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes it. number_sets.py — five nested boxes, left to right, with set symbols, names, and example members. Each box grows in turn. Arrows connect adjacent sets to show the subset relation.

props.code:
# number_sets.py — Claude Code output
SETS = [("ℕ","Natural","{1,2,3…}"),
        ("ℤ","Integer","{…−2,−1,0,1…}"),
        ("ℚ","Rational","{p/q}"),
        ("ℝ","Real","{+irrationals}"),
        ("ℂ","Complex","{a+bi}")]
# → draws 5 nested boxes, left to right, with arrows
props.title: number_sets.py

beats.narration_text: Run it. Five boxes appear left to right — Natural, Integer, Rational, Real, Complex. Each box grows from center. Arrows connect adjacent sets. The subtitle confirms the total nesting: N subset Z subset Q subset R subset C.
claude — add one example number in each set ring showing exactly which rings it belongs to: use 3, negative 2, one half, pi, and square root of negative 1

props.command:
claude "add one example number in each set ring
  showing exactly which rings it belongs to:
  use 3, -2, 1/2, pi, and sqrt(-1)"

beats.narration_text: Five numbers placed in their smallest containing sets. 3 belongs to all five sets. Negative 2 enters at integers. One half enters at rationals. Pi enters at reals — irrational, not rational. Square root of negative one is complex only.

The nesting is total: every natural number is an integer, every integer is rational. The only direction that doesn't hold is down — pi is real but not rational, and there is no algebraic shortcut that makes it rational.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The nesting is total: every natural number is an integer, every integer is rational. The only direction that doesn't hold is down — pi is real but not rational,

beats.narration_text: Pick any five numbers from a textbook problem set. Classify each into its smallest containing set. When a number is rational, verify it — write it as p over q explicitly.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Pick any five numbers from a textbook problem set. Classify each into its smallest containing set. When a number is rational, verify it — write it as p over q e

beats.narration_text: Humanitarians AI. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Humanitarians AI, nikbearbrown dot com.
